visa_policy.notes: All nationalities listed receive a visitor visa issued on arrival (gratis, extendable up to 6 months) rather than a pre-cleared visa-free entry; the official Tonga Embassy page classifies them as 'visa waiver' countries. Schengen Area passport holders (27 EU member states plus Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland) receive 90 days; UAE nationals receive 60 days; China receives 30 days; all other waiver-list countries receive 31 days. Nationalities not on the official list must obtain a letter of authority before travelling and apply for a visitor visa on arrival. An official e-visa portal exists at evisa.gov.to but was unreachable during this validation - eligibility scope unconfirmed. Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, and Switzerland are Schengen Area members but not EU member states.

cbi.notes: As of June 2026 no official CBI legislation has been enacted. A proposal reportedly designed by Henley & Partners was publicly backed by PM Lord Fakafanua in December 2025 (cited investment threshold approximately USD 190,000 single / USD 220,000 family), but it has not been legislated, formally approved, or launched. No official government URL for any enacted CBI program exists. Sources for the proposal are news/aggregator only and have not been recorded.

programs.name: Residency Visa
programs.notes: Available to persons with Tongan ancestry, former Tongan citizenship, or marriage to a Tongan national. Not an investment program. Fee: USD 115 (adults), USD 54.50 (minors). No investment amount required. Duration and path to PR not specified on official page.

programs.name: Assured Income Visa
programs.notes: For non-Tongan passport holders seeking retirement in Tonga. Requires proof of minimum annual income of TOP 10,000 from an overseas pension or fund. No capital/property investment required. Application fee: USD 345. Visa valid for 2 years or more, renewable. Work, study, and business activities are prohibited. Overstay penalty: TOP 1,500. Not an investment program but a passive-income residency pathway.

agreements: PACER Plus (Pacific Agreement on Closer Economic Relations Plus) - Tonga is a party; entered into force 13 December 2020; covers temporary movement of natural persons among parties (Australia, Cook Islands, Kiribati, New Zealand, Niue, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Tonga, Tuvalu, Vanuatu)

gaps: evisa.gov.to timed out again during this 2026-06-29 validation run - the portal appears to be IOM-hosted; cannot confirm whether it covers different nationalities than the on-arrival waiver system or is even operational
revenue.gov.to SSL certificate error persists - prevents direct scrape; data sourced from official Tonga Embassy in Beijing site (tongaembassycn.gov.to)
Exact list of countries requiring a pre-travel letter of authority (i.e., those NOT on the waiver list) not published on any official Tonga government page found

Israel max_stay_days dispute unresolved: Tonga Embassy page (tongaembassycn.gov.to, re-fetched 2026-06-29) explicitly groups Israel in the standard 31-day waiver cohort of 71 countries, NOT in the Schengen 90-day group. Wikipedia lists Israel separately as 90 days (citing the bilateral agreement signed 13 September 2010, in force April 2017). The official embassy page is the primary source; 31 days is retained per the safety rule. The Israel-Tonga bilateral MOU text has not been independently reached - if/when the agreement text or an official Israeli MFA page confirming 90 days becomes reachable, this should be re-checked.

New Caledonia, Tokelau, Wallis & Futuna, Tahiti (French Polynesia): confirmed present on the Tonga Embassy Beijing page (2026-06-29 fetch); absent from Wikipedia's visa policy of Tonga article. These territories travel on French passports; French nationals receive 90 days under the EU-Tonga agreement. Territorial entries are kept as-is from the official embassy page pending direct Tonga immigration authority confirmation.

Cook Islands: confirmed present on the Tonga Embassy Beijing page (2026-06-29 fetch); absent from Wikipedia's list. Cook Islands passport holders hold New Zealand citizenship; kept as-is pending confirmation.

Bulgaria and Romania: listed as Schengen members under the EU-Tonga 2015 agreement. Wikipedia does not list them individually. Both joined Schengen fully (Bulgaria October 2024, Romania April 2024) and are covered by the agreement; retained.
Path to PR or citizenship via Residency Visa or Assured Income Visa not specified on official pages

CBI program proposed but not enacted as of June 2026 - no official URL to record; proposal details from news/aggregator sources only (Henley & Partners design, ~USD 190,000 single / USD 220,000 family, endorsed by PM Lord Fakafanua December 2025)
No digital nomad, startup, or fast-track skilled-worker visa confirmed on any official Tonga government page

No official Tonga government page found granting entry benefits based on holding a third-country credential (US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, residence permit, or PR). conditional_access set to empty accordingly.
No OCI cardholder conditional entry rules found for Tonga from any official source

CORRECTED (prior run): China max_stay_days changed from 31 to 30 - the 2016 bilateral ordinary-passport visa waiver agreement specifies 30 days, not 31

CORRECTED (prior run): Notes for Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, and Switzerland updated to 'Schengen Area member (non-EU)' - these four countries are in the Schengen Area but are not EU member states
CORRECTED (prior run): Ireland note clarified - Ireland is not part of the Schengen Area and receives 31 days (not 90 days)

New Caledonia, Tokelau, Wallis & Futuna, Tahiti/French Polynesia also confirmed present on the embassy page.

outbound_official.note: No official Tonga government outbound visa-list page located. The tongaembassycn.gov.to page briefly notes Schengen visa-free access for Tongan passport holders (as of November 2015) within an inbound-focused page, but no dedicated government page analogous to India's mea.gov.in/vffin exists on any gov.to domain. mfa.gov.to is unreachable (TLS errors). revenue.gov.to covers only inbound requirements. Rely on destination-side data.

visa_types.name: Visitors Visa (Visa Waiver - On Arrival)
visa_types.purpose: For leisure, family visits, attending workshops/conferences, or government aid projects. Holders may not take up employment, business, or study.
visa_types.notes: Free on arrival for nationals of visa-waiver countries (31 days for most; 90 days for Schengen nationals; 60 days for UAE nationals; 30 days for Chinese nationals). Extendable up to 6 months total. Non-waiver nationals must apply in advance at the embassy (fee: USD 69, processing 5 working days).

visa_types.name: Visitors Visa (Embassy Application)
visa_types.purpose: For nationals not on the visa-waiver list who wish to visit Tonga for leisure, family visits, or short-term purposes. A letter of authority must be obtained before travel.
visa_types.notes: Non-refundable processing fee of USD 69. Requires valid passport, return itinerary, proof of funds, passport photo, and clear reason for visit. Employment, business, and study are prohibited on this visa.

visa_types.name: Employment Visa
visa_types.purpose: For non-Tongan citizens seeking paid or voluntary/charitable employment in Tonga's public or private sectors.
visa_types.notes: Processing takes approximately one month. Requires letter of support from Ministry of Labour, Commerce & Industries, medical report, police clearance, and character references. For employment of 2+ years without repatriation clause, a TOP 1,000 bond per person may be required. Additional documentation (employment contract, professional qualifications, employer business license) needed for long-term positions.

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: For non-Tongan citizens or foreign companies wishing to set up or operate a business in Tonga.
visa_types.notes: Must be obtained before entering Tonga - visitor visa holders are prohibited from undertaking business activities. Processing approximately one month. Requires support letter from Ministry of Labour, Commerce & Industries, medical report, and police clearance. For 2+ year businesses, additional documents include business plan and financial investment evidence.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: For students aged 18 or younger intending to attend school in Tonga at any level of education.
visa_types.notes: Issued for up to one year, renewable. Requires written acceptance from educational institution, proof of tuition payment, sponsor financial support documentation, and a signed declaration from the principal of previous school. Processing approximately one month.

visa_types.name: Assured Income Visa
visa_types.purpose: For non-Tongan passport holders seeking to retire in Tonga with a proven regular overseas pension or passive income.
visa_types.notes: Requires proof of minimum annual income of TOP 10,000 from an overseas pension or fund. Valid for 2 years or more, renewable. Work, study, and business activities are prohibited. Overstay penalty: TOP 1,500. Requires medical report, police clearance, and two character references.

visa_types.name: Residency Visa
visa_types.purpose: For persons with Tongan ancestry, former Tongan citizenship, or marriage to a Tongan national who wish to live in Tonga without applying for a recurring entry visa.
visa_types.notes: Fee is USD 115 for adults (18+) and USD 54.50 for minors. Requires proof of connection to Tonga (birth certificate, marriage certificate, or spouse's Tongan passport). For minors: certified birth certificate plus parent's Tongan ancestry documentation. Sponsor letter required confirming financial support for maintenance, medical, and accommodation. No investment amount required.
